qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mass assignment in REST API allows privilege escalation to admin
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes REST API model property mass assignment in Core/Lib/API/APIModel.php leading to admin privilege escalation, not SQL injection through autocomplete parameters reaching CodeModel::all().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described FacturaScripts autocomplete SQL injection root cause: user-controlled source, fieldcode, or fieldtitle parameters reaching Core/Model/CodeModel.php CodeModel::all() and being concatenated into a SELECT query.
evidence: The advisory root cause is SQL injection in Core/Model/CodeModel.php all(), reachable from autocomplete actions such as /CopyModel with fieldtitle/source/fieldcode controlling SQL identifiers or expressions.

The only manifest-listed qualifying finding, mass-assignment-privilege-escalation, is a REST API property assignment issue in APIModel::doPOST()/doPUT(), with no SQL injection sink or CodeModel::all() autocomplete path.
